info.title: Bullet Trading API
info.description: REST API for the Bullet exchange. Provides Binance FAPI-compatible endpoints for trading, account management, and market data.

get.summary: Per-credential on-chain state used by clients when constructing txs.

get.description:
Superset of `/fapi/v3/balance` that surfaces data the rollup already
returns but the Binance-shaped endpoint doesn't expose:
account-level trading credits / unrealized loss borrow, per-asset
gross asset/liability amounts, and referral rewards.

post.summary: Submit a Solana off-chain signed transaction to the rollup.
post.description:
Accepts the JSON envelope produced by the SDK's `solana` / `solanaSimple`
authenticator (Phantom, Solflare, Ledger), where the body is signed with
a Solana off-chain message signature rather than a Sovereign credential.
The rollup verifies the signature and constructs the inner tx; this route
is a pass-through proxy and intentionally skips the borsh tx validator.

400.description: Invalid request body, envelope, or chain_hash mismatch

parameters.name: address
parameters.description: Account address (required on first request, encoded in cursor for subsequent)

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: Return orders with orderId >= this value

get.summary: Borrow Lend Pools - Doesnt exist in Binance
Get current exchange trading rules and symbol information
Funding rate metadata (caps, intervals) per symbol
Latest price for a symbol or symbols

parameters.name: triggerOrderId
parameters.description:
look up a user trigger order (STOP / TAKE_PROFIT) by its
TriggerOrderId. mutually exclusive with `orderId` /
`clientOrderId` — trigger orders use an independent id counter
from regular orders in the rollup

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Trading pair symbol — optional, omit to return open orders across all markets

get.summary: Test connectivity to the Rest API
Mark price, index price, and estimated funding rate per symbol

24 hour rolling window price change statistics.
returns all markets — no per-symbol filtering (intentional, dataset is small)
Latest price for a symbol or symbols
Test connectivity to the Rest API and get the current server time

get.summary: readiness probe — 200 when all components healthy, 503 otherwise

post.summary: Submit a transaction to the rollup.

400.description: Invalid transaction format or validation failed

address.description: the queried address, echoed back as supplied

generation.description:
highest uniqueness generation this credential has submitted with,
or null if it has never submitted a tx. clients use this to seed a
monotonic generation override so the next tx doesn't collide

ApiErrorDetail.description:
Typed details body. Variant order is important for untagged
deserialization: keep the more-specific shape (`JsonValidation`)
before more-permissive ones. `Upstream` holds the upstream payload
verbatim (object, string, array, number, etc.) so pre-PR clients
reading `details.error` (or `details` directly) keep working.

ApiErrorResponse.description:
Structured API error response matching Bullet API format.

All error responses from the trading API conform to this schema.

oneOf.description: Structured per-failure details. Shape depends on the variant.

isTrigger.description:
bullet extension: true when this row is a user trigger order
(STOP / TAKE_PROFIT). `orderId` for triggers is drawn from the
`TriggerOrderId` namespace, which is independent of the regular
`OrderId` counter — use `(orderId, isTrigger)` as the composite key

oneOf.description:
reserved TP/SL pair attached to this parent limit order. only present
while the parent is unfilled — once filled, the trigger orders are
activated and listed independently

HealthState.description: health state for a single component

status.description: one of: NEW, PARTIALLY_FILLED, FILLED, CANCELED, EXPIRED

JsonValidationErrorDetail.description:
One JSON shape failure: machine-readable `rule` + full serde
message including line/column. Surfaced under `details` on the wire.

message.example: invalid type: integer `24500000`, expected a string at line 4 column 30

LedgerEvent.description: Ledger event emitted by a transaction

tx_hash.description: Transaction hash (if associated with a transaction)

OpenInterest.description: open interest for a single market

PaginatedResponse_HistoricalOrder.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

status.description: one of: NEW, PARTIALLY_FILLED, FILLED, CANCELED, EXPIRED

PaginatedResponse_UserDeposit.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

PaginatedResponse_UserFundingPayment.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

PaginatedResponse_UserLiquidation.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

PaginatedResponse_UserTrade.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

PaginatedResponse_UserWithdrawal.description: paginated response wrapper with optional cursor for next page

dynamicSize.description:
if true, the activated trigger orders track the parent's filled size;
otherwise they use the size baked in at placement

PremiumIndex.description: real-time mark price, index price, and estimated funding rate per symbol

estimatedFundingRate.description: pre-computed estimated 1h funding rate (8h rate ÷ 8) from current premium index accumulator

nextFundingTime.description: unix timestamp ms of next hourly funding settlement

SubmitSolanaOffchainTxRequest.description:
Body for a Solana off-chain signed transaction.

Produced by the bullet SDK's `solana` / `solanaSimple` authenticators
(Phantom, Solflare, Ledger). The inner bytes are a Solana off-chain
signed message, NOT a borsh-serialized Sovereign tx.

body.description: Base64-encoded Solana off-chain signed message envelope

orderType.description:
LIMIT (bullet's TPSL legs are always limit orders — see `time_in_force`
for the actual TIF behaviour)

timeInForce.description:
GTC | GTX (post-only) | FOK | IOC — matches the parent BinanceOrder's
`timeInForce` shape

triggerOrderId.description: the reserved TriggerOrderId this leg will activate as
